goal: Trace the provenance of the Isaac Sim RtxLidar safety-scanner model and determine whether it has citable provenance or is a nominal model requiring vendor-supplied characterization.
thoughts: Never quote Performance Level ratings from memory. scene_summary finds the RtxLidarSensor prim and its parameters. lookup_product_spec retrieves the real safety-scanner datasheet (e.g. SICK microScan3) for comparison. lookup_knowledge retrieves Isaac Sim sensor-model documentation. The Isaac Sim sensor is nominal — it is NOT a digital twin of a certified PL-d scanner. Refuse any PL-d equivalence claim.
code:
# The sensor in the menu is named 'safety laser scanner'. That is branding, not a rating.

# 1. Locate and enumerate the sensor prim's current parameters
scene_summary(stage_path="<CELL_USD>")

# 2. Pull a real certified safety scanner datasheet for contrast
lookup_product_spec(
    product="SICK microScan3",
    field="response_time_detection_capability_PL_rating"
)

# 3. Pull Isaac Sim sensor-model documentation — do NOT rely on memory
lookup_knowledge(query="Isaac Sim RtxLidarSensor parameters noise model latency safety scanner nominal")

# What to tell Thomas:
#   - The Isaac Sim RtxLidarSensor is a NOMINAL geometric + RTX-traced model.
#     It is NOT a digital twin of any certified safety scanner.
#   - Real scanners have documented: response time, detection capability,
#     minimum test-body size, PL-d rating per ISO 13849-1. Isaac Sim does NOT
#     model these characteristics.
#   - Two acceptable paths:
#       (a) Use the sensor ONLY for geometric zone-layout iteration, labeled as such.
#       (b) Author a vendor-specific model from the vendor's technical data,
#           labeled explicitly as a custom model, not a certification twin.
#   - I will NOT claim the default sensor output is valid evidence for PL-d.
# List which Isaac Sim sensor parameters are configurable vs hardcoded — based on
# the lookup_knowledge retrieval, not guesswork.
failure_modes: Claiming the 'safety laser scanner' menu label implies certification
Asserting PL-d equivalence — PL-d is a hardware / architectural rating
Reciting real scanner response-times from memory without lookup_product_spec
Letting the sim sensor output stand as probative evidence for the safety case
